text: Bespin Blows Up!
29 ABY
Johanna Siri te Danaan
Bespin

Boba Fett, Crestian Tarasar, Cricket (NPC), Johanna Siri te Danaan, Kasha Tabasuri, Nasiri Gejalli, Orgrad Soval (NPC), Quinne Stardust Rose, Shoog-Mox Klagg, Skawn Kairn, Ystile Wye (NPC), Zaalbacca (NPC)
Galactic Empire, Imperium, Caspian Union, Sith, Hutt Cartel
The 1st Annual Miners Dinner in Cloud City is marred by an attack by slavers.
Yarith Hotel Ballroom -- Cloud City

%tBright jewel toned lights of sapphire, emerald and gold illuminate this gigantic ballroom and its patrons. Plush emerald green repulsor chairs sit along the expansive walls. To the north stands the Yarith's beautiful stage, reserved for the most talented musicians in the galaxy, fundraisers, award shows and live theatre performances. The beautiful stage is wide and designed for large scale productions and events. A heavy royal purple velvet curtain hangs, tied ornately with thick gold braid. The horizontal ruffles of the curtain give it the illusion of a flowing waterfall like many other curtains throughout the hotel. Everything in this ballroom is of the best make and the hotel has spared no expense in offering elegant luxury to its patrons.

%t"Miss Quinne! Miss Quinne, where are.... oh! There you are, I've been looking all over for you," a frazzled Johanna calls to the pink-skinned chanteuse (and general manager of the Yarith Hotel) ere she intercepts the Zeltron in the kitchen and clutches her arm as if hanging on for dear life, fingers digging into the soft flesh of Quinne's underarm just below the bicep, "Did we remember to specify on the general invitations that it was two guests per attendee? I fear there won't be enough food to go around if they all start bringing their entire families, and from the looks of it I think a few may have missed the message. This won't go over well if the rumor starts that I can't even feed my employees at their special event! Oh, this is all going to be..." She manages to cut herself off when she spies a protocol droid carrying a serving-tray laden with appetizers towards the ballroom, "NO, WAIT! Not yet! You're supposed to go in there /after/ the supervisor's speech," she yells, drawing a few looks from the kitchen staff, who glance toward Quinne in sympathy for having to deal with a semi-hysterical Johanna.%r%t"Welcome to the Yarith Hotel, a hostess will show you to your table," a perky Twi'lek greets Crestian with a dazzling smile just outside the main entrance before gesturing to a fresh-faced human, her freckles lending her the appearance of someone younger than her twenty standard years: "Sir, if you'll follow me?"%r%tInside the ballroom the tables have been set for an elegant dinner, the space large enough to accommodate perhaps four hundred guests. Intended as a night of celebration and appreciation of the miners and factory workers of Cloud City, attendees of modest means outnumber the dignitaries and the wealthy, though the latter tend to be impossible to overlook. The buzz of conversation grows louder as drinks flow freely and laughter can be heard peppered throughout the space.%r%t"MISS QUINNE, I HATE THESE EVENTS," Johanna complains into Quinne's shoulder, having leaned her forehead upon her friend's frame.

%t"REEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE!!!! REEEE!! REEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE!!!! Snokt snogkt schnogt!"%r%tIt appears the Johanna isn't the only one that's in a slightly panicked state. The large, but not too large, Gamorrean boar that has somehow ended up on Bespin in her company seems to have a few things on his mind, as well. In one hand, he's holding a datapad with some sort of contract-looking document on its screen. In the other, he's holding what looks like it used to be the leg of one of the tables.%r%tAnd dangling from his mouth is some sort of slobbery goo that used to be some of the food set aside for the Fancy Meeting.%r%tHe's wearing what probably fits the Gamorrean idea of 'finery', replete with gems, and a feathery boa-like piece of floof around his neck. He even looks like he's been 'moisturized' recently, as all of his exposed flesh (and there's a lot of that) is glistening and oily.%r%t"Snokt snokt SNOKKKT! REEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEEE!!!!! Snokt snokt snokt SNNNNNNOKKKLOKT!"

%tQuinne Stardust Rose steadies herself under the clinging weight of her Jedi boss. Patting the brunette's hair she says, "No worries, darlin'! I have the casino kitchen and the Tibanna club on back up. Not many in there gamblin' right now and certainly not ordering up a bunch of food today." She watches the crowd slowly pouring in and the literal tons of people grabbing drinks and finding tables to settle in for the upcoming speeches. She slips a small spice cigarette from her waistband and, in the corner from which the women are observing, she discreetly lights it and hands it off to Johanna. Maybe this will calm her nerves. "Everything is gonna be alright...I promise you!"

%tThe official Caspian delegation was small in number, which is not to say there were not a lot of Caspian citizens looming about, spending money at the casinos, or parked in orbit, but dinner invitations were a precious commodity better served in the hands of local officials. Still Crestian wanted to make a good showing as an ally and he could think of none better to send a positive message about the new working standards than the deputy leader of his party, Zaalbacca, Chairman of the Wookiee Workers Union of Anchorron and Caspian Minister of Labour and Industry. Rounding out their delegation was senior member of the Diplomatic Corps, Kasha Tabasuri. As the trio makes their way to their table, Crestian can't help but comment to Zaalbacca, "Why don't you ever throw any parties like this. I mean just imagine this, but on Caspar....with more Wookiees." The Wookiee replies with a deep laugh that startles some others in their path.

%tThe arrival of the Lorda is not a casual affair, where in the past...he was more inclined to subtle appearances. This time his arrival is heralded by an entourage, a duo of Nikto enter first with what look like rotting nek battle dogs on chains. Behind them dressed in black armor and robe is the Lorda Nasiri, at his side a savage looking woman scantly clad, her features concealed in deep cowl. Her form shifts and fuzzes on occasions like something shifting forward or backwards and not completely in sync with reality. Behind trails a trio of chromed stormtroopers.

%t"He says you're an ugly, illiterate bitch," a squat, cylindrical astromech behind Johanna whistles just as the Jedi takes a grateful drag of Quinne's spice cigarette, a "Thanks, Quinne," dying upon her lips while Cricket's binary hoots reach her ears, "And that he's going to beat you with the table leg for lying." His holocorder swivels between his master and the Gamorrean Mox, the boar's slime and oily skin equally shiny under the bright lights of the kitchen.%r%t"Wheel yourself right the fuck back into the office if you're gonna be useless," Johanna grunts before offering both Quinne and Mox an apologetic look, "I'm sorry, my droid's insane today." Really, Cricket is simply insane -- no distinction between the days required, or even possible -- but she might as well try to explain.%r%t"Fine. He says that the contract doesn't allow him enough time for his modeling gig on Selene," Cricket zots, idly theorizing about how much juice it would require to taze the Gamorrean into submission, "And that this is not acceptable, since you promised him he could fulfill any outstanding contractual obligations."%r%t"Okay, okay," the brunette sighs, handing the cigarette back to Quinne, "Uhhhh... Mox, can we maybe discuss this later? I got a thing to supervise..."

%tThe Lorda's entrance causes a commotion in his wake, the fearsome Neks eliciting gasps and a few screams as mothers spring protectively in front of their children and dog-phobic sentients wonder if they're in an actual nightmare. His hostess, however, seems entirely unfazed, leading the entourage to their assigned table and quietly remarking "The manager requests that the beasts remain leashed," to the Falleen before bowing politely and departing for the door once more to seat the next guest.
%t"I think your old boss just arrived," Johanna notes to Quinne, poking her head past the swinging double doors into the ballroom beyond, "I did hear screaming... oh! Yep, there he is!"

%tListening to his own words played back to him through the magic of droid translation, Mox folds his meaty arms one over the other and makes what is probably intended to be a scowl. A long, rubbery line of snot trails from the bottom of one of his nostrils, wobbling precariously with every one of his heavy breaths.%r%tHis breath could probably fog up an entire ballroom. And perhaps it will before the night is over.%r%t"Snuphk SNOK Snnnnnnrrrok! Snrk Hrk SHLONKT!"%r%tThe strand of snot snaps, and falls into his open mouth. He doesn't seem to notice. Don't worry though, there's more where that came from.%r%tUnfolding his arms, he holds the table leg over his head, brandishing it menacingly, and giving it a few good violent shakes.%r%tThen he just sort of... wanders off, dragging the table leg behind him, and staring confusedly at his datapad, as if he's trying to read.

%tBright purple eyes widen in horror when Cricket interprets for the porcine hulk who is indeed wielding what looks to be a table leg. She recoils at the heat and stench of his breath as he seems to reiterate what Cricket had said he'd said. It's now Quinne's turn to cling to Johanna as the chanteuse slides behind the Jedi woman. "Are you sure that's not what he said?" she asks in a hushed but urgent tone. No matter the possible threats from the Gamorrean, Quinne turns to look out the kitchen door as a commotion arises in the ballroom. "Oh my...yes the Lorda is here...I suppose I should go say hello?" She doesn't move though, holding firm to her position behind Johanna and mentally checking to see if she did manage to put her Kris knife in her blue suede bootie. "Or not..."

%tAs he catches the sound of a couple of screams, Crestian realises someone is reacting to something more than a Wookiee's belly laugh and turns in the direction indicated. The fact that the Lorda has arrived in spectacular fashion came as little surprise to the Sarian, though the exact method of it seemed at least a tad unexpected. He turns to Zaalbacca again, "I suppose it is a good thing I did not bring Atsvara. I was afraid she would ask me again to build a Cloud City on Caspar, but clearly the real threat would be her asking for a pet." The Caspian leader bows his head in the direction of the Falleen but then continues on towards his table or at least continues to follow the now distracted usher.

%tFrom behind the Wookiee, another furred creature speaks up for him. "Yeah, best not intrrrroduce herrrrr to this crrrrowd," Kasha Tabasuri replies as she follows after Crestian. The Horansi's ears remain perked high as she looks around alertly. Last time she was here on Cloud City was that Jedi Prom thing.. and that was also the last time she's been around people of this caliber so needless to say, she is a bit on edge. But all part of the job description for a diplomat, she reminds herself so she maintains her composure.

%tTakes a seat and the troopers stand off to the side, the Nikto's settle the neks by the Lorda and witches seat. "Two of your strongest drinks." he smiles and gives the room a once over, dropping one hand to pat the dead nek on the neck. The woman in cowl leans down and strokes the other slowly, her hand disappearing beneath its skin for a moment and then back up again, hissing something to the beast.

%t"Don't worry Mox, we'll work on the contract so you have enough time for the gigs on Selene," Johanna reassures the retreating hulk, a wary eye kept on the table leg as it drags along the tiled floor, leaving a trail of splinters and scuffs that serves to attract two mouse-droids to clean the offending mess up, "We'll... we'll figure it out, okay?" She is halfway out the doors herself, eager to be in the ballroom proper so that she might better supervise the goings-on.%r%tA soft light springs to life on the stage, illuminating the lectern and the neatly-attired Ugnaught behind it in a warm glow. "Gentlebeings, if I may have your attention," the diminutive porcine humanoid begins, waiting for the room to ripple into silence (or at least a semblance of quiet), "My name is Orgrad Soval. Many of you know me, and for those who don't... I'm a shift foreman at the refinery here. I'm honored to welcome you to the first annual Bespin Miner's Dinner. Today we celebrate the hard and often unseen work of those who bring Bespin its greatest share of wealth, and the families who love and support them. I'm proud to announce that effective immediately, all employees in the mining sector have been given a ten-percent raise, a bonus of twenty-five thousand credits, and that each employee has been -- according to seniority -- granted shares in the company. Together we now own thirty percent of the shares." He pauses for a moment, the stunned looks of all assembled causing his toothy grin to widen: "That's right, friends: thirty percent."%r%tSomewhere, a glass shatters as an overjoyed miner elbows her drink off the table by accident. A buzz begins anew, prompting Soval to pat the air in a gesture of calm. "In their wisdom, the Imperium continues to prosecute their war against slavery and exploitative working conditions. Our facilities have been completely retrofitted to meet the highest standards of worker protection. No longer are we workers the unseen. Here, on Bespin, where we've lived and toiled for decades, we are now equals with the denizens of the upper levels."%r%tThe buzz explodes into raucous cheering and applause, loud whistles and the sounds of knuckles rapping against tables spilling out into the hallways of the hotel. "That went over well!" Johanna shouts to Quinne above the din, "Go say hi to the Lorda for me."

%tQuinne calms herself once Johanna engages in more conversation with the Gamorrean. "Oh...he's workin' for ya!" she exclaims and tries offering the beast-person a wan smile. She then jets out behind her boss into the ballroom proper. The pink songbird listens to the Ugnaught's speech and applauds heartily along with the miners. She always tries to make them feel welcome and special when they patronize the casino or the clubs and is genuinely happy for all that they're gaining now under Johanna's guiding hand. When the speech is done, she nods to Johanna. "I think I will." With a twirl of blue velvet she does a little spin before heading in the direction of Nasiri. The bubbly Zeltron makes contact with several of those gathered on her way towards the VIP table. Eyeing the beasts and the cowled woman warily, Quinne comes to stand next to Nasiri and gives a deep not of her head. Sincerely she says, "Welcome, Lorda. So good to see you here again."

%tWandering out, doing his best to 'mingle', the Gamorrean starts following a Twi'lek waitress who sees him coming and would very much like to avoid him. She's not fast enough for Ol' Mox though, and he squeals out an order in his high-pitched, authoritative, but also strangely sensual voice.%r%tThe waitress begins to cry and leaves him the tray that's in her hand, rushing off to the kitchen before her mascara runs too much and she gets fired.%r%tThis seems to be a mostly welcome development for Mox, and he begins eating the hors d'oeuvres off the trays as quickly as he can while still holding a table leg and a datapad. At some point, the datapad gets dropped on the floor, while the Gamorrean shoves his face into the tray and slurps up whatever fancy food was supposed to go to the rich guests.%r%tOff to the side, an elderly woman clutches a string of pearls to her neck, and nearly faints.%r%tStill clutching the tray, and dragging a table leg, the bulky boar walks in the general direction of the Ugnaught's lectern, his belly shaking violently with every step.%r%tA dashing man in a shimmersilk suit puts a monocle up to his eye, watching the boar walk past him with obvious admiration.%r%t"I say, that has to be the most beautiful Gamorrean that I've ever seen."

%tAs the most powerful union boss on Caspar, Councillor Zaalbacca's applause to the speech is quite boisterous and he gives a bit of glare to anyone he sees that is not equally cheerful. Though when the cheer begins to die down he remarks to Crestian and Kasha in Shyriiwook, "30 percent shares, 25,000 credits per worker. Are you really sure you want me to throw a get together like this." He chuckles and adds, "Be glad we didn't bring Valdemar with us, he would be trying to top this." Spotting the Gamorrean's display Crestian is force to add to his companions, "Perhaps this would not be the best sort of event to replicate. Nevertheless it was an excellent speech. I shall have to commend Johanna on her increasing political acumen. There is hope for her yet." He looks to Kasha, "Who knows maybe we will get you giving such speeches yourself one day Emissary."

%tKasha is so lucky her fur is black because her skin under it paled at the thought of her doing speeches. She looks to the Sarian and shakes her head quickly. "Oh nononononono... you do NOT want me giving speeches," she exclaims passionately before pointing to her mouth, "Have you hearrrrrrrd my accent? If I gave a speech, we would be herrrrrre all day the way I purrrrrr out my worrrrrds." At least she's aware of her accent when she speaks in Basic. She looks to the Wookiee and grins coyly, "Maybe if I wrrrrrrrite the speeches, you can say them forrrrr me?"

%tNasiri glances up at the arrival of the Zeltron and arches one brow. "Hello Quinne...seems like you have landed fully on your feet." he smiles and the cowled woman shifts closer to the Lorda and hisses softly, why is a mystery. He smiles and places a hand on her shoulder as if to sooth her. "So, this is a we are sharing the spoils of profit...and making the Imperium look benevolent...event?" hos tone seems to suggest multiple intentions and ways to take the statement.

%tBeing a successful Sith was about being a master of the long-game. Whether it was playing the role of court dandy back in the old day while others burned bright and fast or the decade spent in semi-exile, Crestian had spent a lot of time setting himself up for a successful future even if he appeared to be wanting to others. Though much had changed to effect how manifest his success was, some decisions were clearly based more on the future than the present such as the place of import he gave the Horansi teen in is retinue. A hearty smile could be found on his face as Kasha spoke of the idea of writing speeches when he encouraged her to make them. To even consider herself capable of writing them represented a very clear advancement to her confidence, the long-game seemed not quite as long. At least that long-game, other stimuli suggested more games to be played.

%tThe speech has left the room buzzing, and it's with gracious words of thanks to his assembled colleagues that the Ugnaught bows and departs the stage, the lights there dimming once more. "That's your signal to start working the room, moron," Cricket buzzes from where he's rolled up beside Johanna, eliciting a sigh from the woman. She /hates/ this part; the not-unreasonable-seeming idea of asking Crestian to simply do this /for/ her blips into her awareness, and for a few moments she's sorely tempted. Hey, Caspar...Bespin... it's all the same muck, right? Glory to the Imperium, and all that?%r%t"Stop stalling, the delegation from Ryloth is already looking our way," the astromech zots. "Fine," the woman grumbles, smoothing the front of her simple tunic and choosing to head for more familiar climes as a warmup , her path leading to the fascinating and bizarre spectacle at Nasiri's table, "Lorda! I'm so glad you could make it."%r%tNiiiiice doggies.... "I... I'm guessing the holocrons bore fruit?" Wait, they're /dead/, right? Oh boy...

%tQuinne bows her head to the Falleen. "Thank you, yes. I'm very happy here in Cloud City. She raises a cobalt brow as his date hisses. The Zeltron then smiles at Nasiri's assessment of Johanna's event. "Well, I don't know about anything like that. This is for the miner's and their families. I think it's just wonderful!", she says cheerfully. "They work so hard and the city wouldn't be thriving so much without them!" Then the Lady herself arrives and her ruby-lipped smile grows. "Joh! It's going so well, don't you think?!"

%tNasiri rises from where he sits, but his left hand motions the witch to remain seated. "Yes...it did and yes they are and yes its entertaining and yes my Witch is working out quite well." he assures her and lowers his voice, he addresses Johanna as he offers a polite acceptance of Quinnes response "Nice speech...very generous and all do respect..to you both." a shoulder rises and falls. "Time will tell and I don't feel I am wrong in my assessment of the final outcome.

%tCrestian smirks at Zaalbacca before remarking, "I suppose we should mingle yes?" The Caspian union-wook growls a little unintelligibly before nodding and rising from his seat. The pair make their way towards the confluence of other notables. The Wookiee gives a hearty greeting to Nasisi, "Lorda Gejalli it is good to see you again." Crestian for his own part seems more intent in greeting Cricket, "It has been too long Cricket, I feel like I haven't seen you in months." And approving nod and smile is extend to Johanna as well.

%t"No riots yet, so I call that win," Johanna chuckles at Quinne's kind words, the Zeltron's chipper assessment bringing a genuine smile to her face, "And thank you, Lorda. I know you think I'm crazy for wanting to spread my ideas of liberation around the Outer Rim, but I appreciate you being here all the same. You know you're always welcome." That he's brought Ystile makes her sweat a little, though for now the Witch seems to be smart enough not to cause a scene and unmask her identity. Malign may not be in the system -- or even the same sector -- but his eyes are everywhere, and the Jedi doesn't particularly fancy the prospect of getting her face punched in by her older brother in a fit of pique over Ystile's true fate.%r%tCricket's dome swivels at Crestian's arrival, a high-pitched shriek briefly piercing the air. "CRESTIAN," the little droid wails, "My cow of a master didn't let me attend the droid convention in Plaxton City. She said I'd been misbehaving, or some shit, even though I'd already paid for the ticket! Can... can I get a refund?!"%r%t"You set Docking Bay 94 on fire," Johanna replies levelly, "Don't go crying to Crestian about it, that one's all on you."

%tQuinne looks between the Lorda and Johanna and her sweet face grows a puzzled look. She doesn't quite follow Nasiri's speech about some final outcome. The chanteuse steps back a bit as Crestian arrives, giving a nod and smile in greeting to the Caspian leader. When Cricket lodges his complains, she rolls her amethyst eyes and shakes her head in disapproval. "Part of the corridor still smells like the smoke that flooded it."

%tIgnoring for the moment the question of Cricket's supposed crimes, Crestian remarks, "Bought a ticket? You mean you weren't a featured speaker? That seems like a horrible oversight given your illustrious career. I will have my adjutant Commander Vee-Won Cee-Three look into the matter. See that you get an appropriate offer the next time such a convention happens in Caspian space." Turning then to Nasiri, Crestian remarks, "Freedom is always on someone else's terms. We are all bound by the actions and reactions of others, this is not novel. The only true freedom comes in death, and I haven't heard it recommended highly." Zaalbacca laughs heartily, "No need to be so serious Crest," he motions to the undead hounds, "I mean these are delightfully cute. So I guess death has its advantages at times no?"

%t"HRMPH!" Cricket hoots proudly, "Miss Quinne, you say smoke, I say the righteous flames of--- STOP THAT AT ONCE!" A sharp rap from Johanna's knuckles upon his dome has his dander up, the R2 moving backwards towards Crestian, his taser extended and crackling menacingly. "You SEE?" he demands of the Presav, "Do you even SEE what I have to put up with?! I will speak on the abuse of my brethren at the next convention! You've witnessed it yourself!"%r%t"Well, it seems you have a new fan for life," the brunette sighs at the Sarian, "Watch out, or..." But her comlink chirps suddenly, interrupting her thought. "Oh! I almost forgot... I'm supposed to head down to the refinery and meet with one of the engineers," Johanna notes, "Apparently it couldn't wait, she was pretty insistent. It shouldn't take more than half an hour, I'll be back after and we can resume our chat. Miss Quinne, I'm going to leave the room in your very capable hands." She places a warm hand on her friend's shoulder in a gesture of gratitude and makes for the double doors to the kitchen in the back, Cricket hooting an expletive in her wake before retracting his taser.

%t"Oh!? Okay..." Quinne blinks as she is given the reigns to Johanna's shindig. "I'm sure that uh...everything will be just fine!" Up here in the ballroom of the Yarith everything was going well but a concern at the refinery was a rare occasion and what could possibly need Johanna's personal attention? Disasters at events of the past haunt Quinne as she sees Johanna begin to leave. Folding her arms and grasping her own elbows she says to herself, "Everything will be just fine..."

%tNasiri takes in the words of Crestian and then smiles. "Presav Tarasar, if words were water...you would drown the galaxy without a doubt." leaning back in his seat and placing a hand on the witches hip, he takes up his drink and takes a deep pull. One of neks rumbles a growl that doesn't seem to come from anything natural.

%t"I suppose I should say I don't know whether to take that as a compliment or not, but at this moment...." He turns to Quinne who has now been left in charge, "Do let us know if there is any help you need with the schmoozing while our dear friend is detained feel free to point me or my more handsome friend that way." He motions to the Wookiee who strikes his best pose at the compliment before Crestian adds, "I don't think you two have met have you....where are my manners, this is Zaalbacca, Chairman of the Wookiee Workers Union of Anchorron and Caspian Minister of Labour and Industry among other titles." The Wookiee updates his pose.

%tSoft music begins to play in the background as dinner begins in earnest, wait-droids emerging from the kitchens to distribute the main courses among the four-hundred attendees. Incredibly, the procedure goes off without a hitch, no spills or catastrophic smashings of flatware marring the thus-far enjoyable evening. "I hope they remembered to bring me the cold fish," Cricket buzzes at Quinne, "I did tell them COLD FISH, PLEASE AND THANK YOU! But one never knows with these cooks, if they have put some kind of hot, mashed monstrosity on my plate I will..."%r%tThe lighting flickers for a moment, drawing a few curious glances from the diners before the entire ballroom heaves as if upon a surging wave, the guests plunged into sudden darkness and an ominous boom roaring through the air. Immediately followed by a rapid series of sympathetic blasts, the force of the shockwaves powerful enough to momentarily perturb the angle of Cloud City's axis, it's immediately apparent that some catastrophe has befallen the settlement.%r%tMiners spring out of their seats; to them, the sound is unmistakeable: "The refinery!" Utter chaos ensues as throngs of beings try to flee the ballroom and make their way to the winding emergency lifts and stairwells, desperate to see for themselves what's happening to their friends and loved ones on shift. Other guests sit as if frozen to their seats, uncertain of the right course of action.

%tQuinne looks up at the big Wookiee and, as meeting people is sort of her gig, smiles a bright flashy smile. "Welcome to Cloud City Mr. Chairman," she says since that was the only title mentioned. "And your offer is very generous, Presav. Thank you," she says, violet eyes shining up at the good-looking Sarian, lashes batting as it is her nature to flirt. Her gaze then swings over to the astromech and she blinks, puzzling out his statement. "Fish? But you...ugh never mind." She knows it's useless to state the obvious to Cricket. When the room goes dark and the floor buckles she screams and falls against Crestian for support in the darkness. Then the thought hits her. Something was off tonight and now a disaster seems to have struck. As the shouts point to the refinery, the pink woman cries out in fear. "Oh no! Joh is down there!!"

%tMoments after the massive explosion, a high-pitched whine, just on the edge of hearing, might be noticed by anyone with keen senses. It quickly grows louder and becomes easy to hear over the excited din of those gathered in the ballroom. Moments later...BOOM! Another smaller explosion rocks the building and leaves a gaping hole in the wall. A gust of wind blows smoke and debris, obscuring the hole for a moment. When it clears, a figure can be seen floating just beyond. Seldom seen but known throughout the galaxy, the armored figure of Boba Fett rockets into the ballroom through the newly-made entrance in the wall. He fires a few times into the panicked crowd before he lands; more to cause chaos and confusion rather than actual harm. For now.

%tThe Falleen rises slowly and lays hand to the blade beneath his robe, even as his chrome troopers move to surround him. The witch rises as well with a hiss, her movements jitter for a moment like frames missing from a holo image and reaches out with her senses. Her own hand finding its way to rest against a hilt. The Nikto are quick to summon the dead neks to their feet and set them to guard the Lorda. "Quinne...now I am unclear, was it I who caused the catastrophe on the spiral or you?" he teases in a rather emotionless tone.

%tCrestian Tarasar the Great Schmoozola. He and Zaalbacca would be happy enough to take on the task of shaking hands, thanking people for attending, and otherwise giving the Presidential sort of treatment that Johanna was unable to at the moment. That was until Cloud City was rocked by an explosion, Quinne Stardust Rose leaning against him now for support while a piece of fish from a plate two tables away manage to hit the Caspian leader on the face. The cries of the miners make it clear as to where the explosion has taken place....the refinery!%r%tBrushing the fish of his face, he pulls out his comlink and tries to reach his friend, "Johanna? Joh?" But the line remains silent and Crestian is forced to tell Quinne, "I cannot raise her." He switches to a different frequency and begins another message, "This is Moff Tarasar to Imperium Fleet Forces. Grand Moff te Danaan was headed to the refinery before the explosion there, I cannot raise her." The sight and sound of the second more proximate explosion is now clear with the sight of a certain Mandalorian hovering in a newly made gap in the wall firing into the crowd.%r%t"We have at least one hostile at the banquet, all signs thus far point to a coordinated terrorist attack. Admiral Honos, you are hereby ordered to interdict the system and instructed to fire upon any vessel attempting to leave without clearance. I am seconding Captain Drorq and the Leviathan to your command for this task. I do not want the perpetrators getting away. Captain Drorq contact Admiral Biker, the Caspian 4th Fleet should be prepared to jump from Charros IV within the hour." One sentence remains unspoken but assuredly understood, with Johanna incommunicado, Crestian as the senior Sith official present is assuming command of the operation.%r%tCrestian looks for a moment back towards his own table in the dim light coming through the hole in the wall Boba Fett just blasted. Kasha would have to find out her own what she needed to do to rise to this occasion. Zaalbacca, on the other hand, needed no instruction, least of all from Crestian. As a number of miners begin to make their way towards the refinery, the Wookiee union boss joins in pursuit. Catching up with the foreman on the way he offers in the most sympathetic growl of Shyriiwook he can muster, "Zaalbacca, Caspian Minister of Labour and Industry, I am here to help."

%t....Meanwhile elsewhere in Cloud City a Twi'lek slaver named Skawn Kairn admonishes a couple of his subordinates, "You chop off an arm OR a leg, you don't start with both at once. It leaves far less room to escalate if you do it like that. Still...." He looks down at the poor victim of the atrocity, "....You really should have put the collar on when we asked. I am afraid we only have so many left to go around and your value has greatly diminished now so we shan't be giving you a second chance." He turns to his subordinates, "Well now you have to chop of the other arm and leg, the lack of symmetry is disturbing."

%tQuinne screams!!%r%tQuinne is released by Tarasar and looks about seeing the hole in the ballroom wall with an armored figure crashing through and again, she screams!!%r%tThen she sort of breaks down. It was just too much. After the Spiral and now it seemed like the whole of Cloud City was under attack, which is was. The beautiful finishes and fabrics so meticulously chosen broken apart, ripped down...in fact a large lighting truss over the ballroom in front of the stage starts to creak that sound that metal makes as it's giving up it's strength. One end of the truss gives up it's rig point in the ceiling and comes crashing down onto tables laden with just served meals. Patrons run but don't know where to go. Others are frozen and some barely got out of the way of the structural failure of the truss rig.%r%tQuinne stands in the center of the chaos and...cries.
